reviewerIndependence.mutationPolicy: The reviewer treated the proposal, supplement, schemas, and authoritative registries as read-only and wrote only this review artifact.

structuralChecks: Strict-compiled the four formal Draft 2020-12 schemas used by the referenced records.
Validated the five referenced work records, five referenced source records, ten supplemental claims, and five supplemental observations against their formal schemas.
Resolved every work, version, source, fact-claim, and interpretation-claim edge and checked the proposed IDs for collisions with the authoritative claim and observation registries.
method.substantiveRule: A proposed observation is approved only when its cited evidence records, rather than merely requests, a reproducible action-to-state-to-feedback-to-consequence trace and directly satisfies every material gate of the target principle.
method.statusRule: A verified source record proves that the registered source is acceptable; it does not automatically verify a new claim made from that source. A prospective runtime-trace instruction is not an observed runtime trace.
method.externalEvidencePolicy: Public corroboration was treated only as lead discovery. It was not used to replace the missing registered original-version runtime traces, and no inference from an unregistered public source was counted as approval evidence.

structuralResults.finding: The supplement is structurally well formed. These checks do not close its factual or causal evidence.

targetPrinciples.principle: New understanding, not new avatar power, makes an old place actionable.
minimumDirectEvidence: The successful action is physically available before knowledge.
No item, capability, consumable, form, song, route flag, or map-layer grant is required between failed and successful runs.
A wrong probe is recoverable and the learned relation causes success at the same target.

targetPrinciples.principle: Several causally distinct paths can satisfy one explicit world-state invariant.
minimumDirectEvidence: At least two controlled paths begin from an equivalent reset state.
The paths differ causally rather than cosmetically or by an exploit.
Both independently reach the same explicit postcondition and neither is eliminated by resource or availability constraints.

targetPrinciples.principle: Optional discovery rewards the player with additional future choices, not only immediate value.
minimumDirectEvidence: Skipping the discovery preserves required progression.
The reward is persistent or repeatably available.
At least two later non-dominated choices become actionable or materially more legible, and choosing one preserves the other.

findings: The north-west-south-west mechanic is plausible, but the evidence edge names a runtime experiment to perform and supplies no captured original-FDS result, trace identifier, timestamp, save-state fingerprint, or before/after state ledger.
The supplement does not demonstrate the exact reset behavior after an incorrect prefix or show that the correct sequence exits from the same maze state.

The method says to consult the in-game clue between runs while also requiring matched snapshots. It does not record whether obtaining that clue changes currency or another player state, nor does it specify a reload that restores the pre-clue snapshot while retaining only player knowledge.
Consequently, action-available-before-knowledge, zero capability or route-flag delta, and recoverable wrong-probe gates remain assertions rather than evidence.
requiredClosure: Record an original-FDS trace with an identifiable maze start, one wrong-prefix reset, the complete successful sequence, and matched inventory, capability, quest, route, and geometry state.
Record the clue transaction or use a documented reload protocol so any non-knowledge delta is explicit and cannot explain success.

findings: The cited locator identifies only the broad Kokiri-Forest-to-Sacred-Forest-Meadow route. It does not enumerate the tested junctions, wrong doorway, successful doorway sequence, or an objective capture of relative melody behavior.
The stronger wording that melody grows clearer at every productive exit and fades at an unproductive one is not established by a recorded original-Nintendo-64 trace.
No paired state ledger proves that age, songs, inventory, capabilities, progression flags, and junction graph were unchanged between failed and successful attempts.
Without the failed return and successful traversal results, the record does not directly prove that sensory knowledge alone, rather than campaign state, opens the route.
requiredClosure: Capture the exact original-version junction sequence with synchronized audio and position evidence, including at least one wrong exit and its reset destination.
Record the before/after player and world-state snapshot and show that the complete route is physically available before any new song, item, age, or progression grant.

findings: The supplement does not record an original-version fork at which the spicy-meal ingredients, cooking access, Old Man ingredient exchange, and Warm Doublet are all demonstrably available before leaving the Great Plateau.

It does not capture the Warm Doublet acquisition conditions or show that Path B can be completed from the declared fork without first using Path A, another cold-protection method, or a progression state absent from the comparison run.
The shared Keh Namut activation postcondition is well specified in prose but is not backed by two recorded outcomes from equivalent objective state.
Path cost, duration, persistence, and resource requirements are not measured, so the assertion that both are viable non-dominated causal paths is also open.
requiredClosure: Record the exact pre-departure availability and acquisition chain for the Warm Doublet in the original version, including inventory, quest, objective, and resource state.
Run meal and clothing branches from a documented fork and record identical Keh Namut activation state, causal independence, rollback, and non-domination evidence.

findings: First repeatable two-tile Suthorn Forest cliff after Trampoline Echo acquisition is not a unique locator: it has no map coordinate, adjacent landmark, captured frame, route timestamp, or other identifier that a reviewer can independently resolve.
The supplement does not record that Old Bed, Table, and Trampoline Echoes are simultaneously learned at that exact point or that the declared Tri power budget supports both constructions.
It gives no exact Old Bed count or placement geometry and no captured Table-plus-Trampoline launch result, so neither route to the same measured upper-ledge region is demonstrated.
Because start position, placed-echo reset, collision result, and postcondition are not recorded, causal distinctness and local reversibility remain inferred.
requiredClosure: Replace the relative locator with a uniquely identifiable original-version location and capture its start and target regions.
Record Echo acquisition state, power budget, exact placements and costs, reset procedure, and successful Old-Bed and Table-plus-Trampoline routes to the same postcondition.

findings: The supplement does not identify the tested campaign and quest state or provide a skip trace proving that Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower is optional while required progression remains reachable.
Persistent regional map data and repeatable launch are asserted without a captured before/after map state or a second launch from the activated tower.

The locator does not name the two outbound destinations, their feasibility constraints, or their separate endpoints. Two visible headings are insufficient unless both are non-dominated routes whose outcomes remain independently reachable.
No before/after comparison shows that activation creates or materially exposes at least two later choices rather than merely offering altitude or convenience for routes already equally actionable.
No branch trace shows that choosing one destination preserves the other, so the required persistent future-choice delta is unproven.
requiredClosure: Record a precise original-version campaign state and a tower-skip route to a required goal to prove optionality.

Capture unactivated and activated map states, repeat launch availability, and two named non-dominated destination routes from a matched apex state, including proof that the unselected route remains reachable.
Measure the choice-set delta attributable to the tower reward rather than inferring it from visibility alone.

blockerDetails.finding: All ten claim locators are prospective runtime-test descriptions. The registered Tier-A source records contain generic primary-game or product-page metadata, but the supplement supplies no archived result, trace fingerprint, timestamp, save-state ledger, or independent reproduction for any proposed claim.
blockerDetails.closure: Attach reproducible original-version trace results and independent review; do not infer claim verification from source-record status.

blockerDetails.finding: The Lost Woods sequence, wrong-input reset, and knowledge-only delta are not jointly recorded, and the clue-between-runs transaction is not reconciled with the matched-snapshot requirement.
blockerDetails.closure: Capture both traversals and explicitly isolate player knowledge from currency, inventory, capability, quest, route, and geometry changes.

blockerDetails.finding: The exact Lost Woods junctions, audio comparison rule, wrong-return behavior, successful route, and zero progression delta are not recorded.
blockerDetails.closure: Provide synchronized original-Nintendo-64 audio and route evidence plus paired state snapshots.

blockerDetails.finding: Warm Doublet availability and causal independence at the proposed Great Plateau fork are not established, and the two claimed routes are not recorded to the same shrine postcondition.
blockerDetails.closure: Record acquisition availability and two controlled, viable, non-dominated branches to identical Keh Namut activation state.

blockerDetails.finding: The Suthorn ledge locator is not unique, and Echo availability, budget, placements, two route outcomes, and reset equivalence are unverified.
blockerDetails.closure: Use a uniquely reproducible location and capture both exact construction routes from the same reset state.

blockerDetails.finding: Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower optionality is not tied to a tested campaign state or a successful skip route.
blockerDetails.closure: Demonstrate required progression without activation at the same campaign state.

blockerDetails.finding: No two named, feasible, non-dominated outbound branches or persistent unselected option are recorded, and no choice-set delta is attributable to tower activation.
blockerDetails.closure: Record the before/after choice set and two distinct route outcomes from the repeatable launch.
